name: Watch
desc: A wearable timepiece designed to be worn on the wrist, typically featuring a strap and a circular or square face showing the time.

name: Mobile Phone
desc: A portable smartphone used for communication, internet browsing, and running various applications, usually shown with a black or silver casing.

name: Laptop
desc: A portable personal computer with a folding screen and keyboard, commonly used for work, study, and gaming in various environments.

name: Keyboard
desc: A standard input device for computers featuring a full set of alphanumeric keys, used for typing text and executing software commands.

name: Desktop Computer
desc: A stationary computer monitor typically used in an office or home setup, representing high-performance computing and professional workspaces.

name: Printer
desc: An external hardware device that takes electronic data from a computer and generates a hard copy on paper using ink or toner.

name: Computer Mouse
desc: A handheld pointing device used to navigate graphical user interfaces on a computer screen by detecting two-dimensional motion.

name: Joystick
desc: An input device consisting of a stick that pivots on a base, primarily used for controlling flight simulators and classic arcade games.

name: Computer Disk
desc: A small optical or magnetic storage disc, often representing legacy data storage or specialized audio recording formats like MiniDisc.

name: Floppy Disk
desc: A 3.5-inch magnetic storage medium that was common in the 1990s, now globally recognized as the universal icon for saving a file.

name: Optical Disk
desc: A silver compact disc or DVD used for storing digital data, music, or high-definition video, reflecting light in a rainbow pattern.

name: DVD
desc: A digital versatile disc used for high-capacity data storage, specifically for feature-length films and software installations.

name: Camera
desc: A classic digital or film camera used for capturing still photographs, often depicted with a large lens and a shutter button.

name: Camera with Flash
desc: A camera shown at the moment of taking a picture, with a bright white burst of light emerging from the built-in flash unit.

name: Video Camera
desc: A portable camcorder used for recording moving images and audio, often used to represent filmmaking or home videos.

name: Movie Camera
desc: A vintage cinema camera with two large film reels on top, used to symbolize the film industry, Hollywood, and professional cinematography.

name: Film Projector
desc: An optical device that projects moving images from a film strip onto a screen, often found in traditional movie theaters.

name: Film Frames
desc: A strip of celluloid film with individual frames and sprocket holes, representing movies, cinema, and video production.

name: Telephone Receiver
desc: The handheld part of a traditional landline telephone, featuring a speaker for the ear and a microphone for the mouth.

name: Pager
desc: A small telecommunications device used to receive short alphanumeric messages, common among medical professionals before smartphones.

name: Fax Machine
desc: A device used to send and receive scanned printed material over telephone lines, typically showing a piece of paper emerging from the top.

name: Television
desc: A classic television set with a screen, buttons, and rabbit-ear antennas, used to represent broadcast media and entertainment.

name: Radio
desc: A portable transistor radio with a tuning dial and antenna, used for listening to music, news, and emergency broadcasts.

name: Studio Microphone
desc: A high-quality microphone used in radio stations or recording studios for professional vocal capture and podcasting.

name: Level Slider
desc: A sliding control found on a mixing console, used to adjust the volume or intensity of an audio signal in music production.

name: Control Knobs
desc: Rotary dials used on electronic equipment to adjust various parameters like EQ, gain, or frequency in audio and video engineering.

name: Compass
desc: A navigational instrument with a magnetic needle that points north, used for hiking, travel, and finding one's direction.

name: Stopwatch
desc: A specialized timepiece used to measure the amount of elapsed time from a particular starting point to a finishing point.

name: Timer Clock
desc: A kitchen or laboratory timer used to count down a specific interval of time, often featuring a red shaded area for the remaining time.

name: Alarm Clock
desc: A traditional bedside clock with two bells on top that ring loudly to wake a person up at a designated time.

name: Mantelpiece Clock
desc: An ornamental antique clock designed to sit on a shelf or fireplace mantel, representing tradition and the passage of time.

name: Hourglass Done
desc: An hourglass where all the sand has fallen into the bottom bulb, indicating that the allotted time has completely run out.

name: Hourglass Not Done
desc: A classic time-measuring device with sand flowing from the top bulb to the bottom, representing time currently in progress.

name: Satellite Antenna
desc: A parabolic dish used for transmitting or receiving signals from satellites, representing telecommunications and space technology.

name: Battery
desc: A portable energy storage device used to power electronics, typically shown in green to indicate a full or healthy charge.

name: Electric Plug
desc: A two-pronged power connector used to link an electrical appliance to a wall outlet for power supply.

name: Light Bulb
desc: A glowing incandescent bulb used for illumination, often used metaphorically to represent a bright idea or sudden inspiration.

name: Flashlight
desc: A portable handheld electric lamp used for seeing in the dark, often used for camping or during power outages.

name: Candle
desc: A pillar of wax with an ignited wick, providing a soft flame for light, relaxation, or religious ceremonies.

name: Diya Lamp
desc: An oil lamp made of clay with a cotton wick, traditionally used in South Asian cultures during festivals like Diwali.

name: Fire Extinguisher
desc: A red pressurized tank used to spray chemicals to douse small fires, representing safety and emergency preparedness.

name: Oil Drum
desc: A large cylindrical metal container used for storing and transporting liquid bulk goods, primarily crude oil or chemicals.

name: Money with Wings
desc: A stack of dollar bills with feathered wings, symbolizing money being spent rapidly or high profits taking flight.

name: Dollar Banknote
desc: A single green paper currency note representing the United States Dollar, used to denote cash, wealth, and commerce.

name: Yen Banknote
desc: A paper currency note representing the Japanese Yen, used in contexts involving the Japanese economy or travel to Japan.

name: Euro Banknote
desc: A paper currency note representing the Euro, the official currency of many European Union countries.

name: Pound Banknote
desc: A paper currency note representing the British Pound Sterling, used in contexts related to the United Kingdom.

name: Coin
desc: A flat, round piece of metal used as money, often used to represent savings, currency, or a toss of a coin.

name: Money Bag
desc: A large sack tied at the top and filled with cash or gold, often marked with a dollar sign to represent great wealth.

name: Credit Card
desc: A plastic payment card used for digital transactions, banking, and shopping without the need for physical cash.

name: Gem Stone
desc: A sparkling blue diamond or precious jewel, used to represent luxury, engagement, or high value.

name: Balance Scale
desc: A traditional scale with two pans, used to represent justice, law, or the weighing of evidence and facts.

name: Ladder
desc: A portable set of steps consisting of two parallel sidepieces joined by rungs, used for reaching high places.

name: Toolbox
desc: A sturdy red or metal container used for organizing and carrying various hand tools for repair and construction.

name: Screwdriver
desc: A hand tool with a handle and a metal shaft, used for turning screws to fasten or loosen materials.

name: Wrench
desc: A metal tool with a shaped opening or jaws for gripping and turning nuts and bolts during mechanical repairs.

name: Hammer
desc: A tool with a heavy metal head and a wooden handle, used for driving nails into wood or breaking objects apart.

name: Hammer and Pick
desc: Two tools crossed in an X shape, traditionally used to represent mining, heavy labor, or construction.

name: Pickaxe
desc: A T-shaped hand tool with a hard head and pointed ends, used for breaking up rocky surfaces or digging in hard soil.

name: Axe
desc: A tool with a sharp metal blade attached to a handle, used for chopping wood or as a primitive weapon.

name: Kitchen Knife
desc: A large, sharp steel knife used by chefs for preparing food, or more generally to represent sharpness and cutting.

name: Dagger
desc: A short, double-edged pointed weapon used for thrusting, often associated with fantasy, combat, or betrayal.

name: Crossed Swords
desc: Two longswords crossed in an X pattern, representing battle, war, or historical martial arts.

name: Bomb
desc: A black sphere with a burning fuse, used to represent an explosion, a dangerous situation, or a 'bombshell' announcement.

name: Water Pistol
desc: A toy gun that shoots water, usually depicted in bright green or orange to distinguish it from a real weapon.

name: Shield
desc: A piece of defensive armor held in the hand to block attacks, representing protection, security, and defense.

name: Cigarette
desc: A lit cigarette with a filter and a small trail of smoke, used to represent tobacco use or a designated smoking area.

name: Coffin
desc: A traditional wooden casket used for burial, representing death, funerals, or spooky Halloween themes.

name: Funeral Urn
desc: A decorative vessel used to hold the cremated remains of the deceased, representing mourning and remembrance.

name: Amphora
desc: A tall ancient Greek or Roman jar with two handles and a narrow neck, used for storing wine or oil.

name: Crystal Ball
desc: A glass sphere used by fortune tellers and clairvoyants to predict the future or look into the spiritual realm.

name: Prayer Beads
desc: A string of beads used in various religious traditions for counting prayers, chants, or devotions.

name: Nazar Amulet
desc: An eye-shaped blue bead used to protect against the 'evil eye' or bad luck in many cultures.

name: Barber Pole
desc: A spiraling red, white, and blue pole used to signify a barbershop, harkening back to historical medical procedures.

name: Alembic
desc: An ancient chemical apparatus used for distillation, consisting of a glass flask with a long downward tube.

name: Telescope
desc: An optical instrument used to observe distant objects in space, representing astronomy and scientific exploration.

name: Microscope
desc: An instrument used in laboratories to view objects that are too small for the naked eye, representing biology and research.

name: Hole
desc: A dark, circular opening in the ground or a surface, often used to represent a pit, a trap, or a void.

name: Pill
desc: A capsule-shaped medicine, usually shown as half-red and half-yellow, used to represent health, sickness, or pharmacy.

name: Syringe
desc: A medical needle used to administer vaccines or draw blood, representing medicine, doctors, and immunization.

name: Drop of Blood
desc: A single red droplet of blood, used to represent injury, blood donation, or medical testing.

name: Adhesive Bandage
desc: A small strip of medical tape used to cover minor cuts or scrapes, representing healing and first aid.

name: Stethoscope
desc: A medical device used by doctors and nurses to listen to the internal sounds of a patient's heart or lungs.

name: Door
desc: A wooden door with a handle, representing an entrance, a new opportunity, or leaving a room.

name: Mirror
desc: A reflective surface in a frame, used for grooming or to represent vanity and self-reflection.

name: Window
desc: A glass pane in a frame, allowing light into a building and providing a view of the outside world.

name: Bed
desc: A piece of furniture with a mattress and pillow, used for sleeping, resting, and relaxation.

name: Couch and Lamp
desc: A living room setup featuring a comfortable sofa and a floor lamp, representing home life and comfort.

name: Chair
desc: A simple wooden or plastic four-legged seat with a back, used for sitting at a table or desk.

name: Toilet
desc: A porcelain plumbing fixture used for the disposal of human waste, representing bathrooms and hygiene.

name: Plunger
desc: A tool with a rubber suction cup on a handle, used for clearing blockages in drains and toilets.

name: Shower
desc: A bathroom fixture that sprays water from above for washing the body, representing personal cleanliness.

name: Bathtub
desc: A large container for holding water in which a person may bathe, representing luxury and self-care.

name: Mouse Trap
desc: A spring-loaded wooden device used for catching rodents, representing a trap or a tricky situation.

name: Razor
desc: A sharp blade used for shaving hair from the face or body, often depicted as a safety razor.

name: Lotion Bottle
desc: A pump-action bottle containing skin cream, sunscreen, or soap, used for skincare and hygiene.

name: Safety Pin
desc: A metal pin with a clasp, used for fastening fabric together or as a punk rock fashion accessory.

name: Broom
desc: A long-handled tool with bristles used for sweeping floors or, in folklore, as a vehicle for witches.

name: Basket
desc: A woven container used for carrying laundry, groceries, or picnic items, often shown with handles.

name: Roll of Paper
desc: A roll of white absorbent paper, typically used for bathroom hygiene or cleaning spills.

name: Soap
desc: A bar of solid soap used with water for washing the body or hands, often shown with a few bubbles.

name: Toothbrush
desc: A small brush with a long handle used for cleaning teeth and maintaining oral hygiene.

name: Sponge
desc: A porous yellow tool used for absorbing water and scrubbing surfaces during cleaning or bathing.

name: Fire Extinguisher
desc: A device that discharges a jet of water, foam, or gas to extinguish a fire in an emergency.

name: Shopping Cart
desc: A metal basket on wheels used in supermarkets to transport groceries and purchased goods.

name: Cigarette
desc: A narrow cylinder of finely cut tobacco leaves rolled in thin paper for smoking.

name: Headstone
desc: A stone marker placed at a grave, often featuring the letters R.I.P. to represent death and mourning.

name: Nazar Amulet
desc: A protective charm in the shape of a blue eye, believed to ward off the evil eye and bring luck.

name: Teddy Bear
desc: A plush stuffed toy in the shape of a bear, representing childhood, comfort, and affection.

name: Nesting Dolls
desc: A set of wooden dolls of decreasing size placed one inside another, originating from Russian culture.

name: Magic Wand
desc: A thin black stick with white tips used by magicians to perform tricks or by wizards to cast spells.

name: Piñata
desc: A colorful donkey-shaped container filled with candy, intended to be broken as part of a celebration.

name: Mirror Ball
desc: A sphere covered with hundreds of small mirrors that reflects light in many directions, used at parties.

name: Balloon
desc: A rubber bag inflated with air or helium, used as a decoration for birthdays and festive events.

name: Party Popper
desc: A small celebratory device that explodes to release confetti and streamers when a string is pulled.

name: Confetti Ball
desc: A golden ball that has split open to shower colorful confetti, used for grand celebrations.

name: Red Paper Lantern
desc: A traditional Japanese or Chinese lantern made of paper, often found outside restaurants and bars.

name: Wind Chime
desc: A glass or metal bell that makes a tinkling sound when moved by the wind, common in Japanese summers.

name: Red Envelope
desc: A small red packet containing money, given as a gift during Lunar New Year or special occasions.

name: Ribbon
desc: A decorative pink bow made of fabric, used for wrapping gifts or as a hair accessory.

name: Wrapped Gift
desc: A present inside a box wrapped in colorful paper and tied with a bow, used for holidays and birthdays.

name: Package
desc: A cardboard box used for shipping goods and mail, often taped shut for secure delivery.

name: E-Mail
desc: A digital icon representing an electronic letter, often shown as an envelope with an '@' symbol.

name: Envelope with Arrow
desc: A paper envelope with a downward arrow, representing an incoming message or a successful download.

name: Outbox Tray
desc: An office tray with an upward arrow, representing mail or files that are ready to be sent out.

name: Inbox Tray
desc: An office tray with a downward arrow, representing new mail or files that have been received.

name: Scroll
desc: A long roll of ancient paper or parchment containing writing, representing history, law, or diplomas.

name: Bookmark Tabs
desc: Small colored sticky tabs used to mark specific pages in a book or document for quick reference.

name: Page with Curl
desc: A white sheet of paper with text, where the bottom corner is slightly curled upwards.

name: Page Facing Up
desc: A single white sheet of paper with printed text, used to represent a document or report.

name: Calendar
desc: A single page from a calendar showing a specific date, used for scheduling and planning events.

name: Tear-off Calendar
desc: A daily calendar where pages are removed one by one to reveal the current day of the year.

name: Wastebasket
desc: A small container used for disposing of trash, typically found under a desk in an office or bedroom.

name: File Folder
desc: A yellow tabbed folder used for organizing and storing paper documents or digital files.

name: Open File Folder
desc: A yellow folder that is spread open, representing the act of viewing or accessing stored information.

name: Card Index Dividers
desc: A set of colored tabs used to categorize and organize large sets of index cards or files.

name: Rolled-up Newspaper
desc: A daily publication of news and articles, rolled up for delivery to a doorstep.

name: Notebook
desc: A simple bound book with lined pages used for taking notes, journaling, or keeping a diary.

name: Notebook with Decorative Cover
desc: A notebook featuring a colorful or stylish cover, used for creative writing or personal notes.

name: Ledger
desc: A yellow-covered spiral notebook traditionally used for accounting, bookkeeping, and tracking finances.

name: Closed Book
desc: A red hardcover book that is closed, representing literature, education, and knowledge.

name: Green Book
desc: A green hardcover book, often used to represent nature, science, or simply a collection of books.

name: Blue Book
desc: A blue hardcover book, representing textbooks, manuals, or library resources.

name: Orange Book
desc: An orange hardcover book, part of a set of colorful book icons used for various subjects.

name: Books
desc: A stack of three or more hardcover books of different colors, representing a library or intensive study.

name: Open Book
desc: A book that is spread open to the middle, representing the act of reading and learning.

name: Link
desc: Two links of a silver chain joined together, used to represent a hyperlink or a connection between ideas.

name: Paperclip
desc: A small wire loop used for holding sheets of paper together, also the universal icon for email attachments.

name: Linked Paperclips
desc: Two or more paperclips hooked together, representing interconnected documents or complex organization.

name: Triangular Ruler
desc: A plastic or metal triangle used for technical drawing, geometry, and measuring angles.

name: Straight Ruler
desc: A flat measuring tool with marked units, used to draw straight lines and measure length.

name: Pushpin
desc: A small pin with a large round head, used to fasten papers to a bulletin board or corkboard.

name: Round Pushpin
desc: A red pin used to mark specific locations on a map or to pin a piece of information.

name: Scissors
desc: A tool with two sharp blades that slide past each other, used for cutting paper, fabric, or hair.

name: Pen
desc: A ballpoint pen used for writing or signing documents, often shown with its cap on or off.

name: Fountain Pen
desc: An elegant writing instrument that uses an internal reservoir of liquid ink and a metal nib.

name: Black Nib
desc: The metal tip of a fountain pen, representing literature, professional writing, and calligraphy.

name: Paintbrush
desc: A tool with a handle and bristles used for applying paint to a canvas or wall in art projects.

name: Crayon
desc: A stick of colored wax used for drawing, especially by children in school or at home.

name: Memo
desc: A piece of paper with a pencil writing on it, representing note-taking, checklists, and reminders.

name: Magnifying Glass Left
desc: A lens used to make objects appear larger, representing search, investigation, and zooming in.

name: Magnifying Glass Right
desc: An alternative magnifying glass tilted to the right, used for searching and finding details.

name: Locked with Pen
desc: A closed padlock with a fountain pen, representing digital signatures and secure documents.

name: Locked with Key
desc: A closed padlock with a key positioned in front of it, representing security, privacy, and encryption.

name: Locked
desc: A closed golden padlock, used to signify that something is secure, private, or prohibited.

name: Unlocked
desc: A golden padlock with the shackle open, representing access, freedom, or a lack of security.

name: Old Key
desc: An antique-style skeleton key with a decorative head, representing secrets, history, or new beginnings.

name: Key
desc: A small metal tool used to operate a lock, symbolizing access, security, and the solution to a problem.

name: Hammer
desc: A tool with a heavy metal head and a wooden handle, used for driving nails and construction.

name: Axe
desc: A tool with a sharp blade used for chopping wood, often used to represent manual labor or survival.

name: Pick
desc: A heavy tool with a pointed head used for breaking up soil or rock, especially in mining.

name: Hammer and Pick
desc: Two tools crossed together, used as a symbol for mining, industry, and hard work.

name: Wrench
desc: A metal tool with a shaped opening for gripping and turning nuts and bolts during repairs.

name: Gear
desc: A toothed wheel that works with others to alter the relation between speed and power in a machine.

name: Brick
desc: A rectangular block of clay used in building walls and structures, representing construction.

name: Chains
desc: A series of interconnected metal links, representing bondage, strength, or being joined together.

name: Magnet
desc: A horseshoe-shaped magnet with red and blue poles, representing attraction and physical forces.

name: Alembic
desc: A glass vessel with a long neck used in early chemistry for distillation and experiments.

name: Test Tube
desc: A glass tube used in scientific laboratories for holding and mixing chemicals or biological samples.

name: Petri Dish
desc: A shallow glass dish used by biologists to culture cells or small organisms like bacteria.

name: DNA
desc: A double helix model of DNA, representing genetics, biology, and the fundamental code of life.

name: Telescope
desc: A device for looking at distant objects, especially in space, representing science and astronomy.

name: Satellite
desc: A communication dish used for transmitting data via satellite, representing tech and space.

name: Candle
desc: A simple wax candle with a lit flame, providing light and representing rituals or peace.

name: Light Bulb
desc: An electric lamp used for illumination, famously used to represent a sudden 'Eureka' moment.

name: Flashlight
desc: A portable battery-operated light source used for seeing in dark places or during emergencies.

name: Izakaya Lantern
desc: A red paper lantern used for lighting in traditional Japanese pubs and outdoor festivals.

name: Identification Card
desc: A plastic card with a photo and text used for proving a person's identity and credentials.

name: Credit Card
desc: A card issued by a bank allowing the holder to purchase goods or services on credit.

name: Money with Wings
desc: A stack of dollar bills with wings, representing money being spent quickly or high profits.

name: Money Bag
desc: A large bag tied with string and filled with money, representing riches and financial success.

name: Coin
desc: A single round piece of currency used as a medium of exchange, often shown in gold.

name: Dollar Bill
desc: A single green paper currency note representing one dollar in the United States.

name: Brick
desc: A sturdy red block used as a primary building material in masonry and architecture.

name: Luggage
desc: A suitcase used for packing clothes and personal items when traveling by plane or train.

name: Sunglasses
desc: Darkened eyewear used to protect the eyes from sunlight and to look stylish or mysterious.

name: Thread
desc: A spool of colorful yarn or string used for sewing, embroidery, and repairing garments.

name: Yarn
desc: A ball of wool used for knitting or crocheting warm clothes like sweaters and scarves.

name: Gloves
desc: Hand coverings used to keep fingers warm during winter or to protect hands while working.

name: Coat
desc: A heavy outer garment worn over other clothes to provide warmth in cold weather.

name: Dress
desc: A feminine garment consisting of a skirt with an attached bodice, worn for various social events.

name: Kimono
desc: A traditional Japanese robe with wide sleeves, tied with a sash and worn on formal occasions.

name: Sari
desc: A traditional garment from South Asia consisting of a long piece of fabric draped over the body.

name: Bikini
desc: A women's two-piece swimsuit worn for swimming or sunbathing at the beach or pool.

name: Handbag
desc: A medium-sized bag with handles, used by individuals to carry personal items on a daily basis.

name: Coin Purse
desc: A tiny bag with a clasp closure used specifically for carrying coins and small amounts of cash.

name: Backpack
desc: A bag with straps that go over the shoulders, used by students and hikers to carry heavy loads.

name: Dress Shoe
desc: A formal leather shoe worn by men for business meetings, weddings, or other special events.

name: Sneaker
desc: A comfortable athletic shoe designed for sports, exercise, and casual everyday wear.

name: Hiking Boot
desc: A sturdy, high-topped boot designed for walking long distances over rough outdoor terrain.

name: High-Heeled Shoe
desc: A women's shoe with a tall, thin heel, typically worn for formal occasions or fashion.

name: Sandal
desc: Open-toed footwear with straps, designed to be worn in warm weather or at the beach.

name: Ballet Shoes
desc: Specialized pink shoes with ribbons, worn by ballet dancers for performing on their toes.

name: Woman’s Boot
desc: A tall leather boot worn by women for style and warmth during the autumn and winter months.

name: Crown
desc: A gold headpiece adorned with jewels, worn by a monarch as a symbol of sovereignty and power.

name: Woman’s Hat
desc: A wide-brimmed hat often decorated with a ribbon, worn for protection from the sun.

name: Graduation Cap
desc: A square black hat with a tassel, worn by students during a commencement ceremony.

name: Billed Cap
desc: A casual hat with a rounded crown and a stiff bill in front to shade the eyes.

name: Lipstick
desc: A cosmetic product used to color the lips, representing beauty, glamour, and preparation.
